title: lithocarpus elizabethae
intro: Lithocarpus elizabethae, also known as the Elizabeth's tanbark oak, is a species of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. This plant is valued for its bark, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bark of Lithocarpus elizabethae is said to have medicinal properties that help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, including eczema and acne. It can be prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in herbal remedies to treat coughs and respiratory issues, making it a valuable plant in traditional Filipino medicine.
